calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-05-2026 Assigned To Trial Court

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-05-2026 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to Defendant Maria Almanza

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: D. Carter; Matter not reported

Johnathan Vaknin, Esq. appeared on behalf of Plaintiff Bank of America.
No appearance by Defendant Maria Almanza.

The court reviews the filed documents as evidence submitted by Mr. Vaknin.

The matter is submitted.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Bank of America, and against Defendant Maria Almanza

Plaintiff Bank of America is awarded judgment against Defendant Maria Almanza as follows:

Damages/Principal: $6,160.67
Pre-Judgment Interest: $0
Costs: $358.61

Total Judgment: $6,519.28

Proposed Judgment was submitted and signed by the Court.

Plaintiff STIPULATES to the return of the exhibits.

Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Oct-27-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted

Plaintiff Bank of America N.A.'s motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, is granted. Defendant Maria S. Almanza has not responded to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (Code Civ. Proc., section 2033.280, subd. (a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs is deemed admitted by Almanza. (Id., section 2033.280, subds. (b), (c).)

Plaintiff does not seek sanctions, but Code of Civil Procedure, section 2033.280, subdivision (c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction . . . on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court orders Almanza to pay $25 in sanctions to Bank of America within 30 days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted
